lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is this place?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: These, my friend, are the foundations of the stronghold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They look like roots to me.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Not just any roots Traveller! These were created by gnome mages eons ago, since then they have grown to become a mighty stronghold!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Impressive. What exactly is the problem?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: In the last two months our tree guardians have reported continuing deterioration of the Grand Tree's health. I've never seen this before! It could be the end for us all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean the tree is ill?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: In effect yes. Would you be willing to help us discover what is happening to the tree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't want to get involved.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I understand Traveller. Please keep this to yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'll show you the way back up.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Up here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd be happy to help!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Thank Guthix for your arrival! The first task is to find out what's killing the tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have an idea?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: My top tree guardian, Glough, believes it's human sabotage. I'm not so sure! The only way to know for sure is to talk to Hazelmere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hazelmere is one of the mages that created the Grand Tree. He is the only one that has survived from that time. Take this bark sample to him, he will be able to help!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: The mage only talks in the old tongue, you'll need this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It's a translation book, you'll need it to translate what Hazelmere says. Do that carefully! His words are our only hope! You'll find his dwellings high upon a towering hill, on an island east of Yanille. I'll show you the way back up. Up here.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'll show you the way back up.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Up here.

sections.title: When speaking to King Narnode Shareen again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller, any word from Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the bark sample.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Here's another sample, hang on to it this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the translation book.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Don't worry, I have more.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hazelmere lives on an island just south of the Khazard Fight Arena, Give him the sample and translate his reply.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I just hope he can help us in our hour of need!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Welcome Traveller. I am King Narnode. It's nice to see an outsider.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi! It seems to be a very busy settlement.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: For now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem worried, what's up?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller, can I speak to you in strictest confidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course sire.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Not here, follow me. Down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off now.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Enjoy your stay with us. There are many things to see in my kingdom.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah....Daconia...blah, blah.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah...Daconia...blah, blah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you write this down and I'll try and translate it?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Your highness.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hello Traveller, did you speak to Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I managed to find him.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Do you understand what he said?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: So what did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode must be stopped, he is a madman!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Praise be to the great Zamorak!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any bread? I do like bread.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The time has come to attack!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The tree is fine, you have nothing to fear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must come and see me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The tree needs watering as there has been drought.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grave danger lies ahead, only the bravest will linger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Time is of the essence! We must move quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A man came to me with the King's seal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is no need for haste.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must act now or we will all die!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Time passes us by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I gave the man Daconia rocks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must use force!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Use a bucket of milk from a sacred cow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take this banana to him, he will understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only Adamantite is any use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All with be fine on the third night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must wait till the second night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing will help us now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The tree will die in five days!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Daconia rocks will kill the tree!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Of course! I should've known! Someone must've forged my royal seal. Hazelmere thought I sent him for the Daconia stones!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are Daconia stones?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hazelmere created the Daconia stones. They are a safety measure, in case the tree grew out of control. They're the only thing that can kill the tree.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: This is terrible! The stones must be recovered!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: First, I must warn the tree guardians. Please, could you tell the chief tree guardian, Glough. He lives in a tree house just in front of the Grand Tree.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: If he's not there he will be at his girlfriend Anita's place. Meet me back here once you've told him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok! I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Wait a minute! That doesn't sound like Hazelmere! Are you sure you translated correctly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm...I think so.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'm sorry Traveller but this is no good. The translation must be perfect or the information's no use. Please come back when you know exactly what Hazelmere said.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the bark sample.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Here's another sample, hang on to it this time!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Time is of the essence Traveller!

sections.title: When speaking to King Narnode Shareen again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hello Traveller, did you speak to Glough?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: OK. He lives just in front of the Grand Tree. Let me know when you’ve talked to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You shouldn't be here human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: The Gnome Stronghold is for gnomes alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely not!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: We don't need your sort round here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Can I help human? Can't you see I'm eating?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The King asked me to inform you that the Daconia rocks have been taken!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Surely not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently a human took them from Hazelmere. Hazelmere believed him; he had the King's seal!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I should've known! The humans are going to invade!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Your type can't be trusted! I'll take care of this! Go back to the King.

sections.title: When talking to King Narnode Shareen again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hello Traveller. If you wish to talk to the prisoner go to the top of the tree, you'll find him there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Your highness. Have you any news on the Daconia stones?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It's OK Traveller, thanks to Glough! He found a human sneaking around! He had three Daconia rocks on him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! That was quick!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Yes Glough really knows what he's doing. The human has been detained until we know who else is involved. Maybe Glough was right, maybe humans are invading!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt it, can I speak to the prisoner?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Certainly. He's on the top level of the tree. Be careful, it's a long way down!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hello adventurer. Have you figured out what's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No idea.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: To get to the bottom of this you'll need to search Glough's home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me. Why would you want to kill the Grand Tree?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't tell me, you just happened to be caught carrying Daconia rocks!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: All I know is that I did what I was asked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Glough paid me to go to this gnome on a hill. I gave the gnome a seal and he gave me some Daconia rocks to give to Glough.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I've been doing it for weeks, this time though Glough locked me up here! I just don't understand it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like Glough is hiding something.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I don't know what he's up to. If you want to find out, you'd better search his home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK. Thanks Charlie.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Good luck!

sections.title: When talking to King Narnode again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your highness! I'm concerned about Glough!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Why? Don't worry about him now the culprit has been caught. I'm sure Glough's resentment of humans will pass with time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not so sure.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: If you're really concerned speak to him.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hello Traveller. So, did you speak to the culprit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I did and something's not right!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The prisoner said he was paid by Glough to get the Daconia stones!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That's absurd! He's just trying to save himself!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Since Glough's wife died he's been a little strange. He would never wrongly imprison someone though! Now that the culprit is locked up we can relax. It's sad but I think Glough was right.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Humans are planning to invade and wipe out the tree gnomes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Who knows? You may have to leave soon Traveller! I trust you but the local gnomes are getting paranoid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough! I don't know what you're up to but I know you paid Charlie to get those rocks!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You're a fool human! You have no idea what's going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know the Grand Tree's dying! And I think you're part of the reason.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: How dare you accuse me! I'm the head tree guardian! Guards! Guards!

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: Come with me!

sections.title: When speaking to King Narnode again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Glough is looking for you! Leave on the glider now before it's too late!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't figure this out Charlie!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Go and see the foreman in the Karamja jungle, there's a shipyard there, you might find some clues. Don't forget the password is Ka-Lu-Min; if they realise that you're not working for Glough there'll be trouble!

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Hi. The king said that you need to leave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently humans are invading!

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: I find that hard to believe. I have lots of human friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand it either!

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: So where to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take me to Karamja please.

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Okay, you're the boss! Hold on tight, it'll be a rough ride.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not anywhere for now.

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Okay. I'll be here for when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: So they got you as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's Glough! He's trying to cover something up.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I shouldn't tell you this adventurer. But if you want to get to the bottom of this you should go and talk to the Karamja Shipyard foreman.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Glough sent me to Karamja to meet him. I delivered a large amount of gold. For what? I don't know. He may be able to tell you what Glough's up to. That's if you can get out of here. You'll find him

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: in the Karamja Shipyard, east of Shilo Village. Be careful! If he discovers you're not working for Glough, there'll be trouble! The sea men use the password Ka-Lu-Min.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Charlie!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller please accept my apologies! Glough had no right to arrest you! I just think he's scared of humans. Let me get you out of there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you can trust Glough, Your highness. He seems to have an unnatural hatred for humans.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I know he can be a bit extreme at times. But he's the best tree guardian I have, he has made the gnomes paranoid about humans though.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'm afraid Glough has placed guards on the front gate to stop you escaping! Let my glider pilot fly you away until things calm down around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, OK.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'm sorry again Traveller!

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: That turbulence over the Karamja Volcano was a bit unexpected, and the area round here isn't well suited for emergency landing.

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Still! we're still alive that's the main thing. Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine, I can't say the same for your glider!

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: I don't think I can fix this. Looks like we'll be heading back by foot. I might see if I can find Penwie while I'm here, I believe he's charting the area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the shipyard from here?

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: I think I saw some buildings on the coast east of here while we were crashing. I'd have a look there.

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: Take care adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take care little man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the shipyard from here?

lines.speaker: Captain Errdo
lines.text: It's east of here. I thought I saw some buildings on the coast while we were crashing...

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hey you! What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to open the gate!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I can see that! Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm from the Ministry of Health and Safety.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Never 'erd of 'em.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will respect my authority!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Get out of here before I give you a beating!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough sent me.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hmm...really? What for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're wasting my time! Take me to your superior!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: OK. Password.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ka.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ko.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ke.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Le.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Min.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Men.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Sorry to have kept you.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: You have no idea!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: This ain't a museum! Leave now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave when I choose!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Well you're not on the list so you're not coming in. Go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll just stand here then until you let me in.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: You do that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you going to let me in then?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You bored yet?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: No, I can stand here all day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright you win. I'll find another way in.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: No you won't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I will.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I'm not starting that again. Maybe if I ignore you you'll go away...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite a few ships you're building!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: This is just the start! The completed fleet will be awesome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: No time to talk we've a fleet to build!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hello matey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Tired!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You shouldn't work so hard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite an impressive set up!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: It needs to be. There's no other way to build a fleet of this size!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hello there. I haven't seen you before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm new!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Well it's hard work, but the pay is good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Too busy to waste time gossiping!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Touchy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like hard work.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I like to keep busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where are you sailing?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, just kidding!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Hello there, are you too lazy to work as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something like that.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I'm just sun bathing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: No time to talk we've a fleet to build!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you building?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course not! You're obviously building a boat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I'm getting tired of this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Breaking my back for pennies! It's just not on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that any way to talk to your new superior?

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't realise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Ouch!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: I cut my finger!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Do you have a bandage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: That's ok, I'll use my shirt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Well there's plenty of work to be done, so if you don't mind...

lines.speaker: Shipyard worker
lines.text: Of course. Sorry to have disturbed you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you in charge?

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: That's right, and you are...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough sent me to check on how you are doing.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Right. Glough sent a human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His gnomes are busy.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Hmm...in that case we'd better go to my office. Follow me.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Tell me again why you're here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...Glough sent me?

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: By the way how is Glough? Still with his wife?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, they're getting on great.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Really? That's odd, considering she died last year. Die imposter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Always arguing as usual!

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Really? That's odd, considering she died last year. Die imposter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sadly his wife is no longer with us!

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Right answer. I have to watch for imposters. What's Glough's favourite dish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He loves tangled toads legs.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Our survey said.... Bzzzzzz! Wrong answer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He loves worm holes.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: OK. Just one more. What's the name of his new girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anita.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Well, well, you do know Glough. Sorry for the interrogation but I'm sure you understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, security is paramount.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: As you can see things are going well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: When I was asked to build a fleet large enough to invade Port Sarim and carry 300 gnome troops I said: 'If anyone can, I can.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a lot of troops!

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: True but if the gnomes are really going to take over Gielinor they'll need at least that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take over?

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Of course, why else would Glough want 30 battleships? Between you and me I don't think he stands a chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: I mean, for the kind of battleships Glough's ordered I'll need tons and tons of lumber! Still, if he says he can supply the wood I'm sure he can! Anyway, here's the order for the lumber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK. I'll head off and give this order to Glough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alia.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: You almost had me fooled! Die imposter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elena.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: You almost had me fooled! Die imposter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He loves tangled toads legs.

lines.speaker: Foreman
lines.text: Our survey said.... Bzzzzzz! Wrong answer!

sections.title: Talking to Foreman after obtaining the lumber order

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: I'm afraid we have orders not to let you in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Orders from who?

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: The head tree guardian, he says you're a spy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough!

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: I'm sorry but you'll have to leave.

sections.title: When talking to Femi (if you helped her)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe they won't let me in!

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: I don't believe all this rubbish about an invasion. If mankind wanted to, they could have invaded before now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really need to see King Narnode. Could you help me sneak in?

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: Well, as you helped me I suppose I could. We'll have to be careful. If I get caught I'll be in the cage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, what should I do?

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: Jump in the back of the cart. It's a food delivery, we should be fine.

sections.title: When talking to Femi (if you didn't help her)

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: Why should I help you, you wouldn’t help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm I know, but this is an emergency!

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: So was lifting that barrel! Tell you what, call it a round 1000 gold pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 1000 gold pieces!

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: That’s right, 1000 and I’ll sneak you in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No chance!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK then, here you go.

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: Alright, jump in the back of the cart. It's a food delivery, we should be fine.

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: OK traveller, you'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks again!

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: That's OK, all the best.

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: Now, to get this lot to the Grand Tree!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help?

lines.speaker: Femi
lines.text: No, I'm OK traveller. I can manage from here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what you're up to Glough!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You have no idea human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You may be able to make a fleet but the tree gnomes will never follow you into battle against humans.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: So, you know more than I thought! The gnomes fear humanity more than any other race. I just need to give them a push in the right direction. There's nothing you can do traveller! Leave before it's too late!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode won't allow it!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: The King's a fool and a coward! He'll bow to me! You'll soon be back in that cage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Nardone, I need to talk!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller, what are you doing here? The stronghold has been put on full alert! It's not safe for you here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your highness, I believe Glough is killing the trees in order to make a mass fleet of warships!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That's an absurd accusation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His hatred for humanity is stronger than you know!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That's enough Traveller, you sound as paranoid as him! Traveller please leave! It's bad enough having one human locked up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing Charlie?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I've been better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough has some plan to rule Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I wouldn't put it past him, the Gnome's crazy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some proof to convince the King.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Hmm... you could be in luck! Before Glough had me locked up I heard him mention that he'd left his chest key at his girlfriend's house. She lives just west of the toad swamp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, your highness.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please Traveller, if the gnomes see me talking to you they'll revolt against me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's crazy!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Glough's scared the whole town, he expects the humans to attack any day. He's even begun to recruit hundreds of gnome soldiers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you understand he's creating his own army?!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please Traveller, leave before it's too late!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to stop you, Glough!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You're becoming quite annoying traveller!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Where are that darn key?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Leave human, before I have you put in the cage!

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Have you taken that key to Glough yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still carrying it around.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Oh. Please take it to Glough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Oh hello, I've seen you with the King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm helping him with a problem.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: You must know my boyfriend Glough then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Could you do me a favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Please give this key to Glough, he left it here last night.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Thanks a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... thank you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were right! I searched Glough's chest and found some invasion plans!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Go to the king and tell him! Finally you have proof. Surely King Narnode will believe you now and set me free!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hold tight Charlie!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I'm not going anywhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Your highness, did you think about what I said?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Look, if you're right about Glough I would have him arrested but there's no reason for me to think he's lying.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I found this at Glough's home!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: If these are to be believed then this is terrible! But it's not proof, any one could have made these. Traveller, I understand your concern, I had guards search Glough's house but they found nothing suspicious, just these odd twigs. On the other hand, if Glough's right about the humans we will need an army of gnomes to protect ourselves. So I've decided to allow Glough to raise a mighty gnome army. The Grand Tree's still slowly dying, if it is human sabotage we must respond!

sections.title: When speaking to King Narnode again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please Traveller, take my advice and leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost those twigs you gave me.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Here take these, I don't see how it will help you though.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Hmm. Looks like you can't carry anymore. Put 4 things down and come back to me.

sections.title: Using anything besides the twigs on the pillars
When clicking out of the cutscene

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Pay attention when I'm talking to you!

sections.title: Talking to Glough during the fight

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Just die, human!

sections.title: When talking to King Narnode without finishing the fight

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your highness, it's true about Glough I tell you! He's planning to take over Gielinor!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'm sorry Traveller but it's just not realistic. How could Glough, even with a gnome army, take over?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He plans to make a fleet of warships from the Grand Tree's wood!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That's enough Traveller! I've no time for make believe. The tree's still dying. I must get to the truth of this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anybody?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You really are becoming a headache! Well, at least now you can die knowing you were right, it will save me having to hunt you down like all the other human filth of Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're crazy, Glough!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Bah! Well, soon you'll see, the gnomes are ready to fight, in three weeks this tree will be dead wood, in ten weeks it will be 30 battleships! Finally we will rid the world of the disease called humanity!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think I'll let you get away with it?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Fool... meet my little friend!

sections.title: When you defeat the Black Demon

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Mummy!

sections.title: When talking to King Narnode again

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller, have you managed to find the Daconia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No sign of it so far.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: The tree will still die if we don't find it! It could be anywhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry Your highness! We'll find it!

sections.title: Searching a root with no Daconia rocks
Searching a root with the Daconia rock

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller you're wounded! What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's Glough! He set a demon on me!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: What?! Glough?! With a demon?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough has a store of Daconia rocks further up the passage! He's been accessing the roots from a secret passage at his home.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Never! Not Glough! He's a good gnome at heart! Guard!

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: Sire!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Go and check out that passage!

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: We found Glough hiding under a horde of Daconia rocks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I've been trying to tell you! Glough's been fooling you!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I..I don't know what to say! How could I have been so blind?! Guard! Call off the military training! The humans are not attacking!

lines.speaker: Gnome guard
lines.text: Yes sir!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: You have my full apologies Traveller! And my gratitude! A reward will have to wait though, the tree is still dying! The guards are clearing Glough's rock supply now but there must be more Daconia hidden somewhere in the roots! Help us search, we have little time!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Traveller, have you managed to find the Daconia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this it?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Yes! Excellent, well done!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It's incredible, the tree's health is improving already! I don't know what to say, we owe you so much! To think Glough had me fooled all along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All that matters now is that humans and gnomes can live together in peace!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I'll drink to that! From now on I vow to make this stronghold a welcoming place for all! I'll grant you access to all our facilities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I think!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It should make your stay here easier. You can use the spirit tree to transport yourself, as well as the gnome glider. I also give you access to our mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mine?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Very few know of the secret mine under the Grand Tree. If you push on the roots just to my north they will separate and let you pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Strange!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That's magic trees for you! All the best Traveller and thanks again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You too, Your highness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I warn you now, human: stay out of my affairs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've stopped you before, Glough, and I'll stop you again!
